model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With only 256 fixed training steps, the baseline is likely under-updated; a moderate LR increase is the simplest high-probability way to improve early optimization without adding complexity.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under fixed-step evaluation, increasing batch size lets each optimizer step use more examples, improving data coverage within 256 steps with a minimal and safe change.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"In fixed-step mode, moving to 256 batch size increases sample throughput further and should improve coverage and validation loss if compute and optimization remain stable.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With larger effective batch and short fixed-step training, AdamW often gives better generalization than Adam at the same learning rate and weight decay by decoupling regularization from adaptive updates.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"AdamW slightly regressed versus the prior best, so reverting to Adam restores the better optimizer choice while preserving the successful larger batch configuration.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With batch size already increased, a slightly higher Adam learning rate can improve progress within the same 256-step budget while keeping the script simple.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The higher learning rate regressed validation loss, so reverting to 1e-3 returns to the stronger operating point before exploring other directions.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The model is very small; increasing base channels should improve representational capacity and can lower validation loss within the same fixed-step budget if optimization remains stable.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"After increasing convolutional width, the small classifier hidden layer may bottleneck representation; a modest increase to 64 can improve utilization with limited added complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Capacity increases have consistently improved loss so far; raising base channels from 12 to 16 is the next simple architecture scaling step with high expected upside.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increasing base channels to 16 regressed from the current best, so reverting to 12 restores the stronger capacity/speed tradeoff.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With very short training (256 steps), L2 regularization can underfit; removing weight decay may allow better fit and lower validation loss in this limited-budget regime.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With fixed 256 updates and now-stable optimization settings, increasing batch size further may improve gradient quality and data coverage per step, potentially lowering validation loss.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After moving to batch size 512, a modest learning-rate increase can better match the larger-batch regime and may improve convergence within 256 steps.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Performance improved when scaling LR with larger batch; a further modest increase to 1.4e-3 is a targeted continuation of that trend for the remaining budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Validation loss has improved as learning rate increased in the current large-batch, zero-weight-decay regime; testing 1.6e-3 is a final high-upside refinement.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Increasing to 1.6e-3 slightly regressed validation loss, so reverting to 1.4e-3 restores the current best learning-rate setting.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"For only 256 updates, reducing Adam's second-moment timescale can improve responsiveness and convergence speed; this is a focused final optimizer-dynamics tweak.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}
